summary: REQ-2 (frontend claim flow) appears largely implemented. REQ-1 (backend grantAdmin function with proper access control) is only partially implemented — a dedicated `grantAdmin(principal)` function callable by existing admin/controller is not evidenced; instead a workaround via `assignCallerUserRole` is used. An unrequested hardcoded admin email promotion path was also added.

missing_requirements.requirement: A backend function `grantAdmin(principal)` exists and can only be called by an existing admin or the canister controller.
missing_requirements.reason: No `grantAdmin(principal)` function is visible in the diff. The frontend uses `assignCallerUserRole` instead, which does not match the specified function name or demonstrate the required access-control guard (existing admin or canister controller only).

missing_requirements.requirement: If no admin exists, the first authenticated caller of `grantAdmin` (or a dedicated `claimInitialAdmin` function) is automatically promoted to admin.
missing_requirements.reason: No dedicated `claimInitialAdmin` backend function is evidenced in the diff. The claim path uses `assignCallerUserRole`, and whether it enforces a 'no admin exists' guard on the backend is not demonstrated in the truncated diff.

hallucinated_features.description: Hardcoded admin email promotion: when a user saves a profile with email 'dhimayustudio@gmail.com', they are automatically granted admin role via `saveCallerUserProfile`.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request does not mention any email-based admin promotion path. The non-goals explicitly exclude email-related notification or promotion logic, and no email-matching mechanism was requested.
